notes.text: Verified the source link — website domain registered 2 days ago, highly suspicious.

title: BREAKING: Government to impose emergency lockdown across Maharashtra
content: Viral WhatsApp message claims government will impose a 7-day emergency lockdown starting midnight. No official confirmation from state or central government. Message spreading rapidly across Maharashtra groups.
reasoning: No official government advisory found. Domain registered 2 days ago. Language designed to induce panic. Classic misinformation pattern during election season.

title: Communal violence rumor spreading near Jama Masjid — Delhi
content: User submitted WhatsApp forward claiming large-scale communal violence near Jama Masjid area. Multiple groups spreading unverified claims of casualties and property damage.
reasoning: No police reports or news corroboration found. Spreading rapidly across multiple platforms. High panic index detected. Potential to incite further violence.

notes.text: Initial analysis confirms this is fabricated content targeting the banking sector.
Escalated for immediate RBI and banking authority notification.

title: False bank collapse news causing panic withdrawals — SBI Bengaluru
content: Fabricated article claiming State Bank of India facing imminent collapse and urging customers to withdraw funds immediately. Article designed to look like authentic financial news.
reasoning: SBI published official denial. Website mimics legitimate financial news portals. Content designed to cause bank run. Cybercrime division involvement recommended.

notes.text: Confirmed as satire article misrepresented as real news. Source website is a known satire portal.

title: Viral claim: 5G towers causing illness in Hyderabad neighborhoods
content: Article claiming residents near 5G towers in Hyderabad reporting mysterious illness. No medical evidence provided. Article references unnamed doctors and anonymous sources.
reasoning: No medical records or hospital admissions corroborate claims. WHO and ICMR have repeatedly debunked 5G health myths. Classic anti-technology misinformation pattern.

title: Fake terror attack alert circulating on WhatsApp — Chennai Metro
content: Message claiming imminent terror attack on Chennai Metro system. Claims police have received intelligence tip. Causing widespread panic and affecting metro ridership.
reasoning: Chennai police officially denied any such intelligence. Message format identical to previously debunked terror hoaxes. Coordinated spreading pattern detected across 50+ groups.

title: False vaccine side-effect claims viral on social media — Kolkata
content: Article claiming new COVID booster vaccines causing heart failure in adults over 40. Uses manipulated statistics and misattributed medical quotes to appear credible.
reasoning: ICMR and WHO data directly contradicts claims. Medical quotes attributed to fictional doctors. Classic anti-vaccine misinformation targeting public health efforts.

notes.text: Digital forensics analysis shows image is from 2019 riots in a different country, repurposed.

title: Manipulated riot images being shared as recent Pune clashes
content: Screenshot submission showing images claimed to be from recent riots in Pune. Images show large crowds and burning vehicles. Being shared with incendiary captions.
reasoning: Reverse image search confirms images are from unrelated 2019 incidents. Captions designed to inflame communal tensions. High risk of inciting real violence.

title: Misleading election result manipulation claims — Jaipur
content: Article claiming electronic voting machines were hacked and election results manipulated in recent Rajasthan by-elections. Provides no verifiable evidence.
reasoning: Election Commission formally denied all claims. No audit anomalies found. Article timed to coincide with political opposition campaign. Designed to undermine democratic process.
